text: Fill in the blanks about the nervous system.
questions: The nervous system is divided into the *central* nervous system (brain and spinal cord) and the *peripheral* nervous system (cranial and spinal nerves).
The autonomic nervous system has two divisions: the *sympathetic* (fight or flight) from T1-L2 and the *parasympathetic* (rest and digest) from craniosacral outflow.
In the CNS, myelination is performed by *oligodendrocytes*, while in the PNS it is performed by *Schwann* cells.
A typical spinal nerve is formed by the union of a *dorsal/posterior* root (sensory) and a *ventral/anterior* root (motor).
An upper motor neuron lesion produces *spastic* paralysis with increased reflexes, while a lower motor neuron lesion produces *flaccid* paralysis with decreased reflexes.
